signals.reasoning: BTC now looks like fresh shorts pressing, but the move is still too small to force and the veto regime keeps it watch-only.
ETH is still trend-supported and already moving, which makes it unattractive as a fade and still blocked as a chase long by the event brake.
SOL is effectively flat and not offering a clean continuation or rejection setup here.
ALT is the best squeeze-long candidate on the board: funding is near -0.95 bps and getting more negative, while price is only modestly up, which suggests pressure is still building rather than spent.
LAYER has even more negative funding than ALT, but liquidity is too poor for a serious fresh trade under veto.
MANTA is too illiquid and too wide to justify a real fresh position.
PUMP has enough liquidity to matter, but the setup is too middling to override the event brake.
WLD is strong but not especially crowded, so it stays watch-only rather than a clean fade or a chase long.
REZ is a valid secondary squeeze-long candidate because funding is strongly negative and open interest is still shrinking into an up move, but liquidity limits confidence.
VVV remains a reasonable secondary fade because the move is extended and the character still looks like short-covering rather than durable trend support.
TON is still one of the better future peak-rejection watches, but it has not actually failed yet, so this is watch-only.
